verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 (beta-1,4-galactosyltransferase 1) is a type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase canonically resident in the trans-Golgi where it adds galactose to N-glycans. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to have a minority cell-surface pool in specific contexts: on sperm it functions as a surface receptor for ZP3 during fertilization, and on somatic cells (including lymphocytes and tumor cells) a surface-localized fraction mediates cell-cell and cell-matrix adhesion. The TM anchor with extracellular catalytic domain means when it reaches the PM, the bulk of the protein body faces outward. This is a documented dual-localization / tissue-restricted surface biology rather than baseline classical surface receptor. Contextual buckets: cell_state_induced—not primary; tissue_restricted_surface—yes (sperm); lysosomal_exocytosis—no; dual_localization—yes (Golgi+PM minority pool); stable_surface_attachment—no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether the somatic surface pool is robust enough across tissues for dual_localization vs. tissue_restricted_surface (sperm) being the cleaner call.
